alertConditions: No schedule event exists within the preceding 36 hours
Latest scheduled run completed with a conclusion other than success
Latest scheduled run remains incomplete beyond the conservative delay window
stopConditions: The automation requires credentials, private data, or a paid external service

Healthy checks would create unavoidable noisy daily alerts
It would enable, dispatch, rerun, or edit the workflow
A read-only test cannot verify current workflow state and scheduled-run freshness

Confirm active state and identify the latest scheduled run without opening logs
Verify no workflow, application, or production state changed
Refresh and health-check Runway OS

phaseWorkBlocks.result: Created active automation expense-tracker-daily-plaid-sync-monitor; its unauthenticated public-metadata test identified active workflow 256886458 and successful scheduled run 29640666471 with no alert conditions, while excluding manual dispatch 29627530457.

phaseWorkBlocks.result: Existing smoke and ephemeral all-entity probes passed initialization, isolation, signed-cents, edit, split, and effective-reporting checks. Transaction identity omitted account or source identity and collapsed one legitimate synthetic collision row, recorded as a high financial-data completeness defect. Passing edit, split, isolation, and reporting behavior lacked tracked regression coverage, recorded as a medium risk. Plaid source impact remains unverified for Task 5, and no excluded action occurred.

Generated temporary CSV, PDF, and XLSX inputs plus route and file-flow probes against an ephemeral DATA_DIR

All-entity checks for profiles, preview, confirm, duplicate, undo, vendor-order persistence, exact, review and unmatched matching, aliases, suggestions, category and subcategory consistency, and cross-entity isolation

phaseWorkBlocks.reportBack: Return a sanitized pass, gap, and unverified matrix; ranked findings with acceptance checks; exact synthetic checks; preserved boundaries; and the Task 3 work-block recommendation.
phaseWorkBlocks.durability: Published directly to main under Ryan's separate post-block authorization with skip actions; no PR, merge, or deploy occurred.
phaseWorkBlocks.result: The tracked smoke suite and 60 ephemeral checks passed CSV and PDF handling, upload confirmation, Amazon and Henry Schein parsing and deduplication, order matching, aliases, cleanup, and entity isolation. Vendor-payment matching failed on a missing transaction column, normal vendor imports discarded line items needed by auto-split, and category inference and acceptance escaped categories.md or varied nondeterministically. A medium tracked-coverage gap and low status-only Undo ambiguity were also recorded without repair, product or test changes, protected-data access, live action, or GitHub durability.

phaseTasks.title: Task 1: Inventory the existing repo and preserve pre-existing state
phaseTasks.summary: Record repo identity, planning surfaces, safe checks, live workflow state, and closed data boundaries.
phaseTasks.result: Read-only intake completed; tracked baseline was clean and two pre-existing untracked files were preserved.

phaseTasks.title: Task 2: Install the full Runway OS scaffold on a dedicated branch
phaseTasks.summary: Stage the standard scaffold separately and transplant only new Runway OS surfaces.
phaseTasks.result: Created codex/runway-os-full-install and added PROJECT_STRUCTURE.md plus the complete command-center tree without overwriting existing files.

phaseTasks.title: Task 3: Migrate current project-control truth
phaseTasks.summary: Replace generic scaffold placeholders with Expense Tracker's canonical current state, roadmap, decisions, rules, architecture, issues, and migration map.
phaseTasks.result: Created canonical project-control sources, the source migration map, parked issues, and proposed multiphase roadmap.

phaseTasks.title: Task 4: Adapt the verification contract to the existing repo shape
phaseTasks.summary: Require existing product roots for retrofit profiles instead of scratch-only app and scratch folders.
phaseTasks.result: Health checks now preserve and validate the existing web, core, and scripts product roots.

phaseTasks.title: Task 5: Verify and close the bootstrap
phaseTasks.summary: Refresh and inspect the dashboard, run Runway OS health checks, rerun the synthetic smoke suite, and verify the diff and worktree.
phaseTasks.result: Refresh, health check, synthetic smoke suite, JSON validation, visual inspection, and whitespace checks passed.

phaseTasks.title: Task 6: Review the proposed roadmap and authorize the first operational block
phaseTasks.summary: Confirm or change the proposed phases and approve the exact Phase 1 work block before any external or production mutation.
phaseTasks.result: Ryan accepted Phases 1-5, target-branch durability, and work block 1A with its documented live effects.

phaseTasks.title: Task 7: Commit and push the verified Runway OS baseline
phaseTasks.summary: Stage only the approved Runway OS paths, commit the install branch, and push it without force.
phaseTasks.result: Staged only approved Runway OS paths, committed e9a8e5e, and pushed the branch without force.

phaseTasks.title: Task 1: Re-enable the disabled Daily Plaid Sync workflow with Ryan authorization
phaseTasks.summary: Restore the intended schedule through an explicitly approved external GitHub mutation.

phaseTasks.title: Task 2: Verify one controlled sync execution
phaseTasks.summary: Verify all-entity results and failure reporting without exposing financial row data.
phaseTasks.result: Controlled workflow-dispatch run 29627530457 completed successfully; the sync job and all job steps passed without opening response-body logs.

phaseTasks.title: Task 3: Establish production and demo health baselines
phaseTasks.summary: Capture safe HTTP, deploy, and workflow health checks in Runway OS.
phaseTasks.result: Daily Plaid Sync and Fly Deploy are active; production and demo roots returned HTTP 200.

phaseTasks.title: Task 4: Define recurring operational checks
phaseTasks.summary: Decide what remains manual and what later becomes a monitored automation.
phaseTasks.result: Compared five safeguards and recommended an independent alert-only Codex monitor; recorded top-of-hour cron hardening separately.

phaseTasks.title: Task 5: Add an independent read-only Daily Plaid Sync monitor
phaseTasks.summary: Create a project-local recurring Codex automation that alerts on disabled state, missing scheduled runs, or a non-successful latest scheduled run without workflow writes.
phaseTasks.result: Created and activated expense-tracker-daily-plaid-sync-monitor on a daily 7:00 AM local schedule; read-only scheduled-event validation passed with no alert conditions.

phaseTasks.title: Task 6: Move the daily trigger away from the start of the hour
phaseTasks.summary: Reduce GitHub's documented top-of-hour delay/drop exposure through a separate source-and-release block.
phaseTasks.result: Changed the default-branch schedule to 17 9 * * * through PR 83; Fly Deploy run 29645346441 passed, Daily Plaid Sync remained active, and production plus demo returned HTTP 200.

phaseTasks.title: Task 1: Rebuild the root README for the current architecture

phaseTasks.title: Task 2: Decide the future of PROJECT_KNOWLEDGE.md and plan.md

phaseTasks.result: Replaced both files with concise historical notices, linked current canonical sources, preserved full contents through exact Git recovery commands, and parked the legacy plan's verification gap for Phase 3.

phaseTasks.title: Task 4: Document deployment, data, and side-effect boundaries
phaseTasks.summary: Create current operator guidance without credentials or row-level financial detail.

phaseTasks.title: Task 1: Audit the transaction foundation and three-entity isolation with synthetic data
phaseTasks.summary: Inspect migrations, entity selection, transaction import and deduplication, CRUD/edit/split behavior, reporting-effective transaction semantics, and cross-entity denial using only tracked source, fixtures, and temporary databases.
phaseTasks.result: Work block 3A passed initialization, isolation, signed-cents, edit, split, and effective-reporting probes; found a high transaction-identity collision risk and a medium tracked-regression-coverage gap without implementation.

phaseTasks.title: Task 2: Audit statement and vendor-order import, matching, and categorization workflows
phaseTasks.summary: Cover CSV and PDF source profiles, upload, confirm and undo boundaries, Amazon and Henry Schein ingestion, vendor matching, aliases, suggestions, and category and subcategory handling with synthetic inputs.
phaseTasks.result: Work block 3B passed the tracked smoke suite and 60 ephemeral checks; it recorded three high-risk correctness defects, one medium regression-coverage gap, and one low operator-clarity issue without implementation.

phaseTasks.title: Task 3: Audit dashboard, reporting, exports, subscriptions, and cash-flow behavior
phaseTasks.summary: Check shared reporting rules, split replacement, exclusions, empty states, date filters, recurring detection, balances, and entity-specific output without opening production data.

phaseTasks.blocker: Task 3 audit execution is not authorized until a synthetic fixture boundary, exact included surfaces, stop conditions, and verification are confirmed.

phaseTasks.title: Task 4: Audit planning, weekly, waterfall, and payroll workflows
phaseTasks.summary: Include the parked Short-Term Planning goal, snapshot, budget, payoff, and entity-isolation evidence gap, and use synthetic payroll and planning records only.

phaseTasks.title: Task 5: Audit Plaid and downstream-sync integration boundaries
phaseTasks.summary: Begin with source and synthetic or mocked behavior; any production account access, Plaid action, workflow action, credential use, or downstream write requires separate target-specific authorization.

phaseTasks.title: Task 6: Audit PWA, responsive navigation, public dashboard, and authentication boundaries
phaseTasks.summary: Use tracked source and synthetic or local behavior first without changing authentication, CSRF, credential, encryption, or public-route behavior.

phaseTasks.title: Task 7: Consolidate audit findings into severity-ranked issues
phaseTasks.summary: Record sanitized reproduction steps, observed versus expected behavior, impact, confidence, acceptance checks, and the affected entity or boundary.

phaseTasks.title: Task 8: Confirm the repair order and bounded Phase 4 implementation work blocks
phaseTasks.summary: Prioritize only evidence-backed findings after the relevant audit slices are complete.

phaseTasks.title: Task 1: Repair confirmed reliability and correctness defects
phaseTasks.summary: Implement prioritized fixes in bounded verified work blocks.

phaseTasks.title: Task 2: Expand regression coverage
phaseTasks.summary: Add synthetic tests around repaired workflows and entity isolation.

phaseTasks.title: Task 3: Add safe CI checks
phaseTasks.summary: Create private-repo CI that uses only synthetic data and no live credentials.

phaseTasks.title: Task 4: Deploy an explicitly approved release
phaseTasks.summary: Deploy only after local and CI verification plus a release approval gate.

phaseTasks.title: Task 1: Review desktop, mobile, and installed-PWA workflows
phaseTasks.summary: Evaluate usability and accessibility after core repairs.

phaseTasks.title: Task 2: Polish high-friction journeys
phaseTasks.summary: Improve confirmed friction without widening financial or authentication risk.

phaseTasks.title: Task 3: Finalize runbooks and release evidence
phaseTasks.summary: Make project re-entry, monitoring, and maintenance durable.

phaseTasks.title: Task 4: Complete target and parent durability closeout
phaseTasks.summary: Commit and push target history and record a compact sanitized parent pointer when authorized.

artifacts.name: Work Block 2A Root Project Entry Point Closeout
Work Block 2A-R Root Project Entry Point Release Closeout
Work Block 2B-R Canonical Guidance Release Closeout

recentActivity.text: Completed read-only existing-project intake and identified a disabled Daily Plaid Sync workflow.
Ryan confirmed a full in-repo Runway OS install rather than a thin overlay.
Created codex/runway-os-full-install and added the standard protected-retrofit scaffold without overwriting existing files.
Completed work block 0A with passing Runway OS health, synthetic application smoke, visual dashboard inspection, and whitespace checks.
Ryan accepted Phases 1-5, authorized target-branch commit/push, and confirmed work block 1A with its controlled live effects.
Committed and pushed the verified full Runway OS baseline as e9a8e5e on origin/codex/runway-os-full-install.
Completed work block 1A: Daily Plaid Sync is active, controlled run 29627530457 passed, and production/demo returned HTTP 200.
Recorded work block 1B as confirmed, reconciled the already-completed merge to main, and completed sanitized safeguard research.
Completed work block 1B: matched recurrence to GitHub's 60-day public-repo inactivity rule, recommended an independent alert-only monitor, and proposed 1C.
Ryan authorized committing and pushing the completed 1B command-center closeout directly to main while preserving pre-existing untracked files.
Ryan confirmed work block 1C for a daily project-local read-only monitor with failure-only alerts and no workflow or financial-system mutation.
Completed work block 1C: active automation expense-tracker-daily-plaid-sync-monitor passed its public scheduled-run metadata test without workflow or financial-system mutation.
Committed and pushed the completed 1C command-center closeout as b1742cf directly to main while preserving pre-existing untracked files; the dashboard durability record followed on main.
Ryan confirmed work block 1D for the minute-17 cron edit, ready PR and merge, one resulting production Fly deploy, and a skip-annotated sanitized closeout.
Completed work block 1D: PR 83 merged the minute-17 schedule as 96af7dc; Fly Deploy run 29645346441 passed; Daily Plaid Sync stayed active; production and demo returned HTTP 200.
Closed Phase 1 and activated Phase 2 for just-in-time work-block planning without authorizing documentation implementation.
Ryan confirmed work block 2A for Tasks 1 and 4: restore the root README and sanitized operating boundaries on codex/phase-2-root-docs, then publish a draft PR without merge or deployment.
Completed work block 2A: rebuilt the root project entry point, passed synthetic and Runway OS verification, pushed source commit c249c9b, and opened draft PR 84 without merge or deployment.
Ryan authorized release block 2A-R to merge verified PR 84 to main, observe one automatic production Fly deploy, and publish a skip-actions closeout.
Completed work block 2A-R: PR 84 merged as 6270304, Fly Deploy run 29646390675 passed, production health and root returned HTTP 200, and the closeout was prepared with skip actions.
Ryan confirmed work block 2B for Tasks 2 and 3: retire legacy guidance, establish tracked AGENTS.md as canonical, publish a verified feature branch, and open a draft PR without merge or deployment.

Completed work block 2B: canonicalized agent guidance, retired competing legacy documents with Git recovery paths, parked the Short-Term Planning verification gap for Phase 3, pushed source commit 912c9bb, and opened draft PR 85 without merge or deployment.
Ryan authorized release block 2B-R to publish verified PR 85 to main, observe one automatic production Fly deploy, and publish a skip-actions command-center closeout.

Completed work block 2B-R: PR 85 merged as 216a992, Fly Deploy run 29647452643 passed, production root and health returned HTTP 200, Phase 2 closed, and Phase 3 activated for just-in-time planning only.

Ryan confirmed work block 3A for a synthetic-only Task 1 audit of the transaction foundation and Personal, BFM, and Luxe Legacy isolation; fixes, tracked test expansion, live access, protected data, and GitHub durability remain excluded.

Completed work block 3A: synthetic foundation and entity-isolation checks passed except for a high transaction-identity collision defect; a medium tracked-regression-coverage gap was also recorded, and no repair, live access, protected data, or durability action occurred.

Ryan separately authorized committing and pushing the exact seven-path 3A command-center closeout directly to main with skip actions while preserving product files and pre-existing untracked scripts/sync_prod_to_local.sh.

Ryan confirmed work block 3B for a synthetic-only Task 2 audit from statement and vendor-order ingestion through matching and categorization across all three isolated entities; fixes, tracked fixture or test expansion, live access, protected data, and GitHub durability remain excluded.

Completed work block 3B: the tracked smoke suite and 60 ephemeral checks passed the core import, upload, order-matching, alias, cleanup, and isolation behaviors; three high-risk correctness defects, one medium coverage gap, and one low Undo ambiguity were recorded without repair or live access.

Ryan separately authorized committing and pushing the exact seven-path 3B command-center closeout directly to main with skip actions while preserving product files and pre-existing untracked scripts/sync_prod_to_local.sh.

projectQuestions: What is the smallest coherent synthetic Task 3 audit block for dashboard, reporting, exports, subscriptions, and cash-flow behavior?

issues.title: Recurring workflow inactivity remains possible but monitored
issues.summary: Active automation expense-tracker-daily-plaid-sync-monitor now detects disabled state, stale scheduled runs, unsuccessful runs, and runs incomplete beyond the delay window without automatic recovery.

issues.title: Daily Plaid Sync runs at the start of the hour
issues.summary: Resolved by PR 83: the cron now uses minute 17; the resulting Fly Deploy passed and the workflow remained active.

issues.title: Project documentation contradicts current architecture
issues.summary: Resolved and released through PR 85 as 216a992 by establishing tracked AGENTS.md as canonical and retiring competing legacy guidance in place; Fly Deploy and production HTTP health passed.

issues.title: Short-Term Planning legacy plan exceeds current verification evidence
issues.summary: The feature is substantially implemented, but the legacy plan's dedicated smoke cases and seeded goal and snapshot examples are not present as written and need a fresh Phase 3 audit.

issues.title: Transaction identity can collapse distinct transactions
issues.summary: Synthetic 3A evidence showed that rows sharing date, amount, and description but differing by account receive the same ID, causing one legitimate row to be silently skipped; real-data occurrence is unknown.

issues.title: Transaction edit, split, and effective-reporting paths lack tracked regression coverage
issues.summary: The 3A ephemeral probe passed edit, split, cross-entity denial, and reporting replacement behavior, but the tracked smoke suite does not guard those paths.

issues.title: Vendor-payment matching references a missing transaction column
issues.summary: Fresh Personal, BFM, and Luxe Legacy databases all failed vendor-payment matching because core/vendor_matching.py references transactions.matched_order_id, which no migration creates.

issues.title: Vendor-order imports discard parsed line items
issues.summary: Synthetic Amazon and Henry Schein imports persisted order summaries but no order_line_items rows, leaving matched-order auto-split without the detail it requires.

issues.title: Vendor categorization can escape the category source of truth
issues.summary: Synthetic matching wrote an undefined category in all three entities, mixed-category ties varied across hash seeds, and the categorization endpoint accepted undefined category and subcategory values.

issues.title: Task 2 import and categorization paths lack tracked regression coverage
issues.summary: The 3B ephemeral probe passed 60 parser, upload, matching, alias, cleanup, and isolation checks, but the tracked smoke suite does not guard those paths.

issues.title: Upload Undo label does not explain its status-only effect
issues.summary: The action marks the monthly checklist incomplete but leaves imported transactions intact, while the interface labels it only Undo.

nextMilestone.name: Bounded Task 3 audit decision
nextMilestone.description: Define and confirm the smallest synthetic dashboard, reporting, export, subscription, and cash-flow audit slice before execution.
